title: ADP enrichment part (layer 3)
description: One additive, attributed enrichment anchored to the session. Everything in layer 3 carries attribution (who made it, first- or third-party, trust). A clip is the first-class enrichment object: a time range + intent + labels that references a video asset and the timeline (NOT a footage copy).

enrichment_id.description: Unique id of this enrichment within the package.

session_ref.description: UUID of the logical activity this enriches.

anchor.description: What span of the session this enrichment is attached to.

clip_ref.description: Referenced clip's enrichment_id, when type = clip_ref.

kind.description: What kind of enrichment this is. Open set; known kinds enumerated. 'clip' selects the clip payload contract below.

attribution.description: Who produced this enrichment and how much to trust it. Required on every layer-3 part.

source.description: Upstream data source, e.g. 'openweathermap', 'osm', model name.

trust.description: unverified = ingested as-is; self = producer vouches; reviewed = independently reviewed.

payload.description: Kind-specific body. Free-form for most kinds; when kind = 'clip' it SHOULD match the clip contract in $defs/clip.

clip.title: Clip payload
clip.description: First-class clip: one or more time ranges + intent + labels/tags/description/derived metrics, referencing a video asset and the timeline. Not a copy of the footage.

ranges.description: Time range(s) this clip spans, on the canonical UTC timeline. Multiple ranges allow a stitched/highlight clip.

video_ref.description: Reference to the video asset this clip is cut from (a part name / asset id, NOT embedded footage).

intent.description: Why the clip exists (free text, e.g. 'gnarly rock garden', 'PB segment').

derived_metrics.description: Metrics computed over the clip's range (open set, e.g. max_speed_ms, avg_hr_bpm). These are convenience aggregates carried with the clip.

direction.description: OPTIONAL playback direction. live-trails uses it (POV forward/reverse), rfr does not. Omit when not applicable.
